home.pageTitle: Matej Lukášik - Specialist in actually useful AI
home.heroTitle: Stop doing work that AI should be doing for you.
home.heroDescription: AI should save your business time and money, not create more work. First, we'll find the recurring work that takes up the most time in your business. Then we'll simplify it, automate it, and measure the result.
home.ctaSecondary: See Ways We Can Work Together
home.painTitle: Bringing AI into your company only makes sense when it solves a real business problem.
home.pain1Title: Copying between emails, spreadsheets, and systems
home.pain1Description: Data is manually copied, checked, and passed along. Your team loses time on work that automation can do faster and with fewer errors.
home.pain2Title: Quotes, reports, and replies are still created manually
home.pain2Description: People repeatedly search for the same information, create nearly identical documents, and check them before sending. AI can prepare the first draft while a member of your team keeps control and makes the final decision.
home.pain3Title: AI must not become another experiment without results
home.pain3Description: Before deployment, we define what should improve: time, cost, error rates, or service speed. If we cannot measure the result, we narrow the project or do not start it.
home.consultingDescription: If you are not sure where to start, I will help map opportunities, choose the right first project, and decide how we will measure success.
home.implementationDescription: If you already have a clear problem, I will design and build a practical AI solution that fits your processes, tools, and security rules.
home.trainingTitle: AI in Your Team's Day-to-Day Work
home.trainingDescription: If AI is going to work long term, your team needs to use it in everyday work. I help people find concrete use cases, verify outputs, and build safe work habits.

processSteps.title: 45-Minute AI Opportunity Diagnostic
processSteps.description: In 45 minutes, we walk through one recurring process and assess whether it is worth automating.

processSteps.title: First-Step Plan
processSteps.description: I propose the first process worth solving, a success metric, security rules, and a realistic rollout path.

processSteps.title: Execution
processSteps.description: We build the agreed solution, test it on real work, train the first users, and measure its impact.

processSteps.title: Handoff
processSteps.description: Once the solution works, your team takes it over. If you want to keep going, long-term support remains available.

firstStepOffer.title: 45-Minute AI Opportunity Diagnostic
firstStepOffer.description: In 45 minutes, we'll map one recurring process and assess whether it is worth automating. If it isn't, I'll tell you directly. The diagnostic is free and comes with no commitment.

home.atlasProofTitle: AI agent security is not an abstract risk.
home.atlasProofDescription: I maintain a public atlas of agentic AI incidents that maps real failures to threat categories and prevention principles teams can use before deployment.
home.trustDescription: I recommend the technology and rollout path that fit your business problem, team, budget, and security requirements.
home.ctaBandTitle: Let's start with the process that costs your business the most time.
home.ctaBandDescription: Most clients start with a 45-minute AI opportunity diagnostic. No commitment, no unnecessary hype.

notSure.description: Most clients start with a 45-minute AI opportunity diagnostic. No commitment, no jargon — just a concrete look at one recurring process.

about.heroDescription: I help companies turn AI pressure into safe, measurable solutions.
about.bioText:
I am an agentic AI consultant with 10 years of software engineering experience. I help small and medium-sized businesses find specific places where AI can save time, streamline teamwork, and create measurable value.

I take a practical approach to projects: first, I understand the business problems, processes, and data sources; only then do I choose the right tools. Together we design the first usable workflow, set success metrics, and define safe deployment rules.

My solutions build on experience with LLM systems, RAG, evaluations, AI models, automations, and AI agents. My goal is to deliver a solution leadership understands, the team can use, and the company can keep developing independently.
about.principle1Description: Open source or managed, self-hosted or cloud — I recommend what fits your team, budget, and security requirements.
about.principle2Description: I train your team alongside deployment. The goal is for you to own your AI workflows and keep developing them.
about.principle3Description: First we validate one useful use case. We measure results and expand only what works.
about.techDescription: The right technical solution depends on the workflow. Sometimes a lightweight automation is enough; other times it takes a RAG system or a custom AI agent connected to existing tools. What they share are clear rules, controlled access, measurable results, and a team that knows how to use the solution.
about.ctaTitle: Let's talk about the first AI workflow worth solving.

faq.question: We don't know where to start with AI. Is it worth getting in touch?
faq.answer: Yes. This is the most common starting point. On the first call we look at your processes, data, and the places where the team loses time. The goal is the first process worth solving, not a list of tools.

faq.question: How long does the first deployment take?
faq.answer: Most first projects move from kickoff to a working solution in 2–6 weeks. A simple automation can be live in days. More complex systems take longer, but you should see progress every week.

faq.question: How do we know whether AI is creating real value?
faq.answer: Before building, we define the metric: time saved, shorter cycle time, fewer errors, better quality, or faster customer response. If value cannot be measured, we narrow the project or postpone it.

faq.question: How do you handle data security?
faq.answer: We start with data, permissions, and deployment boundaries. The first version should use only the minimum necessary access, clear rules for sensitive data, and human review where the risk is higher. With security, slower and steadier is better.

faq.question: Do we need our own technical team?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. I can handle the technical side. Someone technical on your team helps with long-term maintenance and ownership, but the first project can start without an internal developer.

faq.question: What tools do you work with?
faq.answer: I choose the tool only after understanding the process. Sometimes a simple automation between the apps you already use is enough. A custom AI agent makes sense only for more complex work. What matters is security, operating cost, and whether your team can keep using the solution over time.

faq.question: What happens after handoff?
faq.answer: Once the solution works, your team gets the operating rules, training, and enough context to use it safely. The goal is capability, not dependency. If useful, we can keep improving the solution together.
